title: Repair the active Codex repo fallback: git-root commands, current wrappers, real installed-route contract tests
action: Repair the live source-repository Codex project fallback without changing plugin/global state. Before editing, rerun `env -u CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR bash -c 'node "$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR"/.bee/bin/hooks/bee-state-sync.mjs'`; require exit 1 plus MODULE_NOT_FOUND for `/.bee/bin/hooks/bee-state-sync.mjs`, and record that exact command/output under `red_failure_evidence` in this cell's verification evidence (the evidence stays in the cell trace, not a new report file). Then run and record `codex plugin list --json` plus a targeted census of project/user hook definitions; if output is unavailable/unparseable, or any bee plugin/second bee hook source is present, return `[BLOCKED]` with ZERO edits. Extend the single logical catalog with a deterministic repo-target Codex rendering whose transport resolves the git root and executes the current shared wrappers under hooks/ with explicit source identity repo; preserve the existing plugin-target Codex projection and Claude projection byte-for-byte (per D1). Root-resolution or launch setup must itself fail open visibly (one diagnostic, exit 0) rather than exit 1 before Node; a wrapper deny exit 2 must remain exit 2 (per D2). Regenerate .codex/hooks.json from that repo-target rendering rather than hand-authoring event groups. Add process-level tests that parse the actual commands from .codex/hooks.json but execute them only inside an isolated temporary git fixture containing copied current wrappers and bee helper state; pin the real repository's .bee/state.json, .bee/.inject-cache.json, and hook logs byte-for-byte before/after the test. Run every configured command with CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR and CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT unset from fixture root, nested cwd, and a fixture path containing spaces plus Unicode. Both Stop handlers must exit 0 and every non-empty Stop stdout must parse as JSON systemMessage with no decision:block. The configured PreToolUse command itself (not a direct wrapper call) must deny a gated apply_patch targeting .bee/state.json with exit 2. Include a root-resolution-failure row that exits 0 with a visible coverage diagnostic. After GREEN, repeat the source census; bee must remain absent and no trust/global configuration may be modified.

truths: the two active Stop commands no longer resolve to /.bee and both exit 0 when Claude root variables are absent
every command in .codex/hooks.json resolves inside this git repository from root and nested working directories
non-empty Stop output is parseable JSON with systemMessage and never decision:block
repo-root resolution or hook launch setup failure emits a visible diagnostic and exits 0, while a computed hook denial still exits 2
the configured PreToolUse transport denies a gated apply_patch targeting .bee/state.json with exit 2
.codex/hooks.json is byte-derived from the shared catalog's repo-target Codex rendering, so hand drift turns the suite red
the existing plugin-target Codex projection and Claude projection remain byte-identical to their pre-cell generated forms
installed-route tests run only in an isolated git fixture and leave the real repository's bee state, injection cache, and hook logs byte-identical
before/after parseable source-census evidence shows no bee plugin or second bee hook source, and unknown/both states block with zero edits

artifacts.substantive: one logical catalog can deterministically render plugin-target and source-repo-target Codex command transports without forking wrapper logic
active trusted Codex project fallback rendered from the catalog and independent of CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR
process-level regression runs the commands Codex actually loads, including nested cwd and Stop JSON assertions

key_links: .codex/hooks.json is reproduced byte-for-byte by the catalog's repo-target Codex renderer
each configured project command resolves to hooks/bee-*.mjs, which imports hooks/adapter.mjs and then the repository .bee/bin/lib helpers
the active-route harness points the configured transport at a fixture git root, never at the live orchestration state
diagnosis-codex-stop-hooks.md is the before-characterization for this behavior change
prohibitions: do not install, enable, disable, trust, or configure any global Codex plugin or hook
do not modify ~/.codex, ~/.claude, .claude settings, plugin manifests, installers, onboarding, or vendored .bee/bin/hooks in this slice
do not fork seven Codex-specific wrapper implementations or duplicate the event catalog by hand
do not weaken existing hook fixture rows or convert advisory Stop output into a continuation decision
do not execute installed-route regression payloads against the live repository's bee state
do not touch E3 dispatch/skill work, E4 docs/release work, or fanout-delegation artifacts

trace.dropped_reason: Rejected at validating (reality gate FAIL): scope overload (>=2 cells), unenforceable byte-identity truth, verify green against the broken config, transport string unspecified. Replaced by split cells codex-parity-6a (catalog repo-target renderer + regenerated config) and codex-parity-6b (process-level installed-route harness). See docs/history/codex-runtime-parity/reports/validation-codex-repo-fallback.md
